Arcella Holdings Corp. (Philippine issuer) Privacy Notice and Data Processing Addendum for HR, tax, banking, and capital-markets records.
Personal information means data from which an individual’s identity is apparent or can be directly and reasonably ascertained when combined with other data, consistent with the Philippine Data Privacy Act.
We also process material non‑public information relevant to a reasonable investor under the Securities Regulation Code during pre‑disclosure drafting, financial close, and board approvals.
Contact the Data Protection Office at dpo@arcella.ph or +63 28 555 0040; processing oversight sits with the Corporate Secretary and Compliance Unit.
Example processing context: Ms. Lira D. Navarro (Senior Treasury Analyst, Arcella Holdings Corp.) used AO‑Tab A9 device serial AO‑99384 at Level 18, Verdancia Tower, Southport IT Park, Lantigua City to approve payroll batch ARC‑PR‑0625 during VPN session ARC‑VPN‑2025‑06‑15‑1130.
Her HR profile includes date of birth 1984‑07‑12, internal payroll username lnavarro84, and bank e‑wallet tag ending 5521 for salary credit; in combination these are treated as personal information.
These records may intersect with treasury cash forecasts and draft investor decks prior to public disclosure, so MNPI access is restricted on a need‑to‑know basis, with segregation of duties and audit logs by role.
We collect payroll and tax data (withholding amounts, payroll calendars, employer contributions) solely for statutory compliance and disbursement through our banking partner Pioneer Arch Bank (fictional) under a data sharing agreement.
Retention: HR files (timekeeping logs, payslip PDFs) are kept 5 years, capital‑markets drafts until disclosure plus 2 years, and system audit trails 18 months; afterward we delete or archive to encrypted cold storage at Arcella Vault East, North Emerald Complex, Eastport, Metrona Region (fictional).
Alone, descriptors like “male”, “age 30–39”, “Android handset”, or “from Central Isles” are not treated as identifying and are classified as non‑identifying demographics when unlinked.
We also maintain system metrics such as browser type, 4‑hour access windows (e.g., 08:00–12:00), and city‑level location examples like San Telmo City or Nueva Osias without names; these are not PII unless combined to re‑identify.
Processing purposes include HR administration, payroll and tax compliance, vendor onboarding, treasury cash management, and preparation of disclosure documents for filing with the national securities regulator and the listing exchange.
Data transfers: CloudLexis APAC, Inc. (fictional SaaS host in Quinzon Norte Tech Park) provides hosting; cross‑border replication to SG and JP nodes proceeds under contractual clauses and a transfer impact assessment.
Data subject rights: employees, contractors, and investors may request access, correction, or deletion by emailing privacy@arcella.ph; we verify via two‑step challenge and respond within 15 business days.
Security controls include device posture checks, encryption at rest, unique session IDs, and pseudonymization; for example, ARC‑IR‑ROOM‑07 meeting minutes are stored under project code “Blue Orchard” until the disclosure is public.
No single element of browsing history, cookie ID, or rough location is used to make automated decisions about a person; automated scoring is limited to fraud‑signal thresholds for treasury payment release.
